sections.paragraph:
θυμόω 1 aor. 3 sg. ἐθύμωσεν Hos 12:15; pass. ἐθυμώθην; fut. θυμωθήσομαι LXX (s. θυμός; Aeschyl., Hdt. et al.; Dio Chrys. 10 [11], 20; LXX; TestSol 20:6; JosAs; ParJer 6:23 [cod. C]; EpArist 254; Jos., Bell. 1, 221, Ant. 12, 270) make angry; pass. become angry. Abs. (Polyb. 5, 16, 4; TestDan 4:4; TestSol 20:6) ἐθυμώθη λίαν Mt 2:16. In GJs 22:1 v.l. for ὀργισθείς.—DELG s.v. θυμός.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

TestDan TestDan = Testament of Dan, s. Test12Patr—List 2

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 462
